title: The One Where AI Learned to Count

cards.headline: AI Milestones This Week in History

events.text: Perceptron unveiled — the first neural network hardware
Deep Blue defeats Kasparov in chess
Google publishes 'Attention Is All You Need' — the Transformer paper
AI agents autonomously ship production code at Fortune 500 companies

cards.headline: 4.7 Trillion
cards.body: Parameters in Meta's Llama 5 — the largest open-source model ever released. That's more parameters than there are stars in the Milky Way (estimated 100–400 billion).

cards.headline: Coding Showdown: AI vs Human
cards.body: On the SWE-bench Verified benchmark, Claude Opus 4 resolves 72.5% of real GitHub issues — outperforming the average senior engineer's first-attempt success rate.

cards.headline: AI Pizza Chef Goes Rogue
cards.body: A food delivery AI was asked to suggest toppings for a 'healthy pizza' and recommended mixing kale, sardines, and gummy bears — calling it 'nutritionally diverse.' The customer was not amused. The AI was not fired.

cards.headline: Cursor Tab — AI Autocomplete on Steroids
cards.body: Cursor's multi-line tab completion predicts not just the next line, but the next 5–10 lines of code based on your patterns. It learns your coding style within hours. Free tier available.

cards.headline: AI Adoption by Industry (2026)
cards.body: Source: McKinsey Global AI Survey, Q1 2026
The best way to predict the future is to build it. The second best way is to ask an AI to predict it and then argue about whether it's right.
cards.author: Andrej Karpathy
